title: viola subandina
intro: Viola subandina, also known as the Sub-Andean Violet, is a flowering plant species native to the high-altitude regions of the Andes. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Viola subandina include the potential treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach ulcers, as well as its use as an anti-anxiety and anti-stress agent. The plant is commonly prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
